title: Fixed #36786 -- Fixed XML serialization of None values in natural keys.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

[ticket-36786](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/36786)

#### Branch description

**AS-IS**:
- `None` values in natural keys were serialized as the string `"None"`, causing deserialization to fail.

**TO-BE**
- `None` values are serialized as `<None/>` elements, consistent with how regular fields handle `None`.

**Before**
```python
# Check for None first - but this matches <None/> inside <natural> tags too!
if node.getElementsByTagName("None"):
    return None
else:
    ...
    keys = node.getElementsByTagName("natural")
    if keys:
        field_value = [getInnerText(k).strip() for k in keys]
```

After

```python
natural_keys = node.getElementsByTagName("natural")
if node.getElementsByTagName("None") and not natural_keys:
    return None
else:
    ...
    if natural_keys:
        field_value = []
        for k in natural_keys:
            if k.getElementsByTagName("None"):
                field_value.append(None)
            else:
                field_value.append(getInnerText(k).strip())
```

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: 2ykwang

reviews.body:
Thanks -- especially appreciate the tests.

Main feedback at this point is to just prioritize a smaller diff over what we might otherwise do if rewriting this from scratch.
Thank you, looks great! 🎁

Fixed #36786 -- Fixed XML serialization of None values in natural keys.

None values in natural keys were incorrectly serialized as the string
"None", causing deserialization to fail for fields like UUIDField.

```

```

Otherwise testing at the schema level looks more appropriate than what I did in #20412
Perfect -- thanks @cliffordgama and @charettes for the review.

commits.message:
Fixed #36800 -- Restored ManyToManyField renaming in BaseDatabaseSchemaEditor.alter_field().

Regression in f9a44cc0fac653f8e0c2ab1cdfb12b2cc5c63fc2.

Now that ManyToManyField is no longer concrete the decision of whether or not
it should be altered, which is also relied on by field renaming, should take
into consideration name changes even if it doesn't have a column associated
with it, as auto-created many-to-many relationship table names are a base of it.

Note that there is room for optimization here where a rename can be entirely
avoided if ManyToManyField.db_table remains stable between .name changes, just
like we do with Field.db_column remaining stable, but since this is a
regression and meant to be backported the current patch focuses on correctness
over further improvements.

Thanks Josik for the report.

Co-authored-by: Simon Charette <charette.s@gmail.com>

title: Refs #27890 -- Avoided overwriting TMPDIR in runtests.py under forkserver mode.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-27890

#### Branch description
Under the forkserver multiprocessing mode (default in python 3.14 for linux), runtests.py was getting executed multiple times, causing nested temporary dirs that didn't clean up properly, raising `FileNotFoundError`. Traced it to an environment var being set multiple times.

E.g. printing the value of `os.environ["TMPDIR"]` after its assignment shows:

```
/var/folders/_c/qsz9054d71v9z4wt9p38wc9c0000gn/T/django_ak1l0rlj
```
and then
```
/var/folders/_c/qsz9054d71v9z4wt9p38wc9c0000gn/T/django_ak1l0rlj/django_v2jzvuwm
```

Ultimately giving:
```py
Exception ignored in atexit callback <function rmtree at 0x1030b2980>:                                                            
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/shutil.py", line 852, in rmtree
    _rmtree_impl(path, dir_fd, onexc)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/shutil.py", line 721, in _rmtree_safe_fd
    _rmtree_safe_fd_step(stack, onexc)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/shutil.py", line 799, in _rmtree_safe_fd_step
    onexc(func, path, err)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/shutil.py", line 760, in _rmtree_safe_fd_step
    orig_st = os.lstat(name, dir_fd=dirfd)
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/var/folders/_c/qsz9054d71v9z4wt9p38wc9c0000gn/T/django_ak1l0rlj/django_v2jzvuwm'
```

Which is similar to the error from ticket-27890, although a slightly different cause. We don't appear to need that older workaround.

To reproduce on Mac, set:
```py
    multiprocessing.set_start_method("forkserver")
```
at the top of the `__main__` block.

See recent [logs](https://github.com/django/django/actions/runs/20120702843/job/57740016165).

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [ ] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [ ] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: jacobtylerwalls

reviews.body: Looks great, thank you for debugging this! 🏅

commits.message:
Refs #27890 -- Avoided overwriting TMPDIR in runtests.py under forkserver mode.

This variable should only be set once. Under forkserver, this module
was getting executed multiple times, causing nested temporary dirs
that didn't clean up properly, raising FileNotFoundError.

This similar to #27890 although a slightly different cause.

title: Refs #36499 -- Adjusted test_strip_tags following Python behavior change for incomplete entities.
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36499

#### Branch description
python/cpython#140904 changed how it parses incomplete entities found at the end of a string, now sending them through the `handle_entityref()` method, which, in our subclass, prepends `&` and appends `;`. This was considered a data loss fix.

Follow-up to 2980627502c84a9fd09272e1349dc574a2ff1fb1. When this specific test case was added, it was under a comment:

title: Fixed #36701 -- Fixed memory leak in ModelState.
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36701

#### Branch description
This PR addresses ticket #36701 regarding memory leaks caused by reference cycles in `ModelState`

I implemented a `__del__` method on `ModelState` to explicitly clear `self.fields_cache`.

I added a regression test (`tests/model_state/test_memory_leak.py`) that:
1. Creates a `OneToOneField` cycle.
2. Deletes strong references.
3. Forces a `gc.collect()`.
4. Asserts that the objects are successfully reclaimed (using `weakref`).

I also verified this locally with a reproduction script generating 50,000 cyclic objects. Without the fix, memory usage balloons. With the fix, `gc.collect()` successfully reclaims the objects.
#### Checklist

title: Fixed #36783: Fixed querystring regression for multi-value QueryDict in 6.0
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36783

#### Branch description
This PR fixes a regression to the `querystring` template tag. As of Django 6.0, only the last value of a multi-value QueryDict is preserved from `request.GET`, rather than the entire list. PR provides failing test (which passes in 5.2.x and fails in main) and fix.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: marcgibbons

reviews.body: Thanks! Please add a release note in 6.0.1.txt.

Thank you @marcgibbons for starting this work, and thank you @jacobtylerwalls for the rounds of review 🌟 

I'm afraid the provided solution can not be merged since it ignores the logic defined in the existing `for key, value in d.items()` loop. I do acknowledge  that `.items()` is loosing params, and that it needs fixing, but IMHO we can't just have a disconnected if-else to fix that.

Some tests that show what I mean:

```diff
diff --git a/tests/template_tests/syntax_tests/test_querystring.py b/tests/template_tests/syntax_tests/test_querystring.py
index 41da747146..5908b770ae 100644
--- a/tests/template_tests/syntax_tests/test_querystring.py
+++ b/tests/template_tests/syntax_tests/test_querystring.py
@@ -118,6 +118,24 @@ class QueryStringTagTests(SimpleTestCase):
         context = RequestContext(request, {"my_dict": {"test": None}})
         self.assertRenderEqual("querystring_remove_dict", context, expected="?a=1")
 
+    @setup({"querystring_remove_querydict": "{% querystring my_query_dict %}"})
+    def test_querystring_remove_querydict(self):
+        request = self.request_factory.get("/", {"x": ["y", "z"], "a": ["1", "2"]})
+
+        qd_none = QueryDict(mutable=True)
+        qd_none["x"] = None
+
+        qd_list_none = QueryDict(mutable=True)
+        qd_list_none.setlist("x", [None, None])
+
+        expected = "?a=1&a=2"
+        for my_query_dict in (qd_none, qd_list_none):
+            with self.subTest(my_query_dict=my_query_dict):
+                context = RequestContext(request, {"my_query_dict": my_query_dict})
+                self.assertRenderEqual(
+                    "querystring_remove_querydict", context, expected
+                )
+
     @setup({"querystring_variable": "{% querystring a=a %}"})
     def test_querystring_variable(self):
         request = self.request_factory.get("/")
@@ -136,6 +154,18 @@ class QueryStringTagTests(SimpleTestCase):
             "querystring_dict_list", context, expe
Thanks everyone! 🌟

commits.message:
Fixed #36783 -- Ensured proper handling of multi-value QueryDicts in querystring template tag.

title: Fixed #36769 -- Avoided visiting deeply nested nodes in XML deserializer.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36769

#### Branch description
This change adds a limit to how deep getInnerTextList() will read XML tags.
Django fixtures only need one level of text (for example: <field><natural>text</natural>).
Deeper levels are not used and can cause performance problems if the XML is very nested.

#### Checklist

I'm imagining a new file in `tests/fixtures/fixtures` like `invalid_deeply_nested_elements.xml` showing that text inside unescaped markup like `Django pony` now just extracts "Django " rather than "Django pony". Then, it will be more easy to tell if we even need the max_depth at all. My preliminary testing is showing that we don't need to recurse at _all_, since we call `getInnerText` directly on `` elements.

title: Fixed #26434 -- Removed faulty clearing of ordering field when missing from explicit grouping.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-26434

#### Branch description
This is a fix for a broken functionality directly related to the use of `queryset.count()` and `queryset.aggregate(...)`, i.e., a fix for the `QuerySet API`.

The issue itself describes the problem well, but in short: the ORM, due to overly aggressive SQL query optimization, removes parts of order_by even for columns that are not logically part of group_by.

One solution was already in master, but it was flawed from two perspectives:
1. Typically, the order_by part is just text, while the group_by part consists of column objects (Col). The previous solution did not reliably compare these two sets.
2. The fix was placed in the shared logic for aggressive SQL query optimization. This is not the right place here - we want the fix to reside in the final top-level aggregation, i.e., the method `query.get_aggregate(...)`.

Please provide feedback on:
1. The tests that have been written.
2. Whether the implementation is headed in the right direction.
3. What is missing in the implementation or what could be improved.
4. Anything else you think is worth discussing.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
author: michalnik

reviews.body:
I really like the idea of creating a clone to resolve the `order_by` without polluting the query with then unused `JOIN`s @michalnik thanks for that!

I left some comments that I think are aligned with the kind of feedback you were requesting, mainly around ways to avoid unnecessary cloning and resolving as these can be expensive.

Thanks for giving this second shot.
The logic seem sane to be, made very cosmetic comments that can be handled by the merger if needs be but otherwise this looks good to go.
Thanks, last remaining question for me just relates to `extra`.
Thank you!

commits.message:
Fixed #26434 -- Removed faulty clearing of ordering field when missing from explicit grouping.

Co-authored-by: Simon Charette <charette.s@gmail.com>

title: Fixed #36233 -- Avoided quantizing integers stored in DecimalField on SQLite.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36233

#### Branch description
Fixed a crash in DecimalField when using SQLite with integers exceeding 15 digits (e.g., 16-digit IDs).

The Issue: SQLite lacks a native decimal type. When retrieving large integers (e.g., 9999999999999999), the default converter treats them as floating-point numbers. Since IEEE 754 floats only support ~15 significant digits, precision is lost (e.g., becoming 10000000000000000). When Django attempts to quantize() this inaccurate value against the field's context, it raises decimal.InvalidOperation.

The Fix: Updated django.db.backends.sqlite3.operations.py to check if the underlying SQLite driver returned a native int. If so, the value is converted directly to Decimal, bypassing the lossy float conversion entirely.

Verification: Added a regression test test_sqlite_integer_precision_bypass in model_fields/test_decimalfield.py to ensure 16-digit integers are stored and retrieved correctly without crashing.
#### Checklist
- [ ] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [ ] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [ ] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.

title: Fixed #36712 -- Evaluated type annotations lazily in template tag registration.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36712

#### Branch description
Python 3.14 introduces [deferred evaluation of type annotations](https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.14.html#whatsnew314-deferred-annotations), which can make headaches around circular definitions go away.

`inspect.getfullargspec` wasn't updated to take the new `annotation_format` arg that was provided to let inspecting libraries (us) avoid eagerly evaluating user annotations, see https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141560.

One solution would have been to copy and paste the entire function--along with importing the seven or eight other symbols it needed--and do the tweak, long-term forking the implementation.

Instead, patching the module helper anticipating the change we want Python to make felt more future-proof and sufficiently self-documenting. However, I'm happy to swap this out for a copy-and-paste approach if that's preferable, but we'd want to redistribute the python license in that case, I suppose?

Ideally, this will be reverted when an upstream solution is available for https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141560.

Thanks Patrick Rauscher for the report and Augusto Pontes for the first iteration and test.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.

- [x] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.

title: Closed pool when parallel test runner encounters unpicklable exceptions.
body:
**Before**
```py
multiprocessing.pool.MaybeEncodingError: Error sending result: '<multiprocessing.pool.ExceptionWithTraceback object at 0x10bbecec0>'. Reason: 'TypeError("cannot pickle 'BufferedReader' instances")'
Exception ignored while calling deallocator <function Pool.__del__ at 0x107aaa610>:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/multiprocessing/pool.py", line 268, in __del__
ResourceWarning: unclosed running multiprocessing pool <multiprocessing.pool.Pool state=RUN pool_size=8>
```
**After**
```py
multiprocessing.pool.MaybeEncodingError: Error sending result: '<multiprocessing.pool.ExceptionWithTraceback object at 0x109c18d70>'. Reason: 'TypeError("cannot pickle 'BufferedReader' instances")'
```


To reproduce, cause a failure in `tests/servers` by doing this:
```diff
diff --git a/django/test/testcases.py b/django/test/testcases.py
index c587f770a6..40e785f5dc 100644
--- a/django/test/testcases.py
+++ b/django/test/testcases.py
@@ -1824,11 +1824,11 @@ class LiveServerTestCase(TransactionTestCase):
 
     @classmethod
     def setUpClass(cls):
-        super().setUpClass()
         cls.enterClassContext(
             modify_settings(ALLOWED_HOSTS={"append": cls.allowed_host})
         )
         cls._start_server_thread()
+        super().setUpClass()
 
     @classmethod
     def _start_server_thread(cls):
```
author: jacobtylerwalls

reviews.body:
Do you know if there is a reason why we don't use `pool` as a context manager?

It looks likes something `__exit__` should handle by itself?

commits.message: Closed pool when parallel test runner encounters unpicklable exceptions.

title: Added timeout-minutes directive to all GitHub Actions workflows.
body: GitHub Actions defaults to a 360 minutes (6 hours) timeout. We've had jobs hang due to issues in the parallel test runner, causing them to run for the full 6 hours. This wastes resources and negatively impacts CI availability, so explicit timeouts have been added to prevent long-running hangs.
author: nessita

reviews.body: Lovely, this also gives us some breathing room to identify the cause of the hanged jobs.

commits.message:
Added timeout-minutes directive to all GitHub Actions workflows.

GitHub Actions defaults to a 360-minute (6-hour) timeout. We've had jobs
hang due to issues in the parallel test runner, causing them to run for
the full 6 hours. This wastes resources and negatively impacts CI
availability, so explicit timeouts have been added to prevent
long-running hangs.

title: Fixed #29257 -- Caught DatabaseError when attempting to close a possibly already-closed cursor.
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-29257

#### Branch description
When ``cursor.execute()`` raises an exception and ``cursor.close()`` also raises, Django used to surface the ``cursor.close()`` error, adding noise to the original execution failure and producing misleading messages such as: "cursor _django_curs_xxx does not exist".

This patch ensures that the original ``execute()`` exception is preserved without unnecessary context. A regression test is included to verify that ``cursor.close()`` errors do not distract from the real underlying exception.

title: Fixed #36112 -- Added fallback in last_executed_query() on Oracle and PostgreSQL.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36112

#### Branch description
This PR fixes the issue described in ticket #36112, where ``DatabaseOperations.last_executed_query()`` in the Oracle backend may return ``None`` when a query fails. This causes SQL formatting to crash under ``--debug-sql``.

Following the core team’s guidance on the ticket (Jacob Walls & Mariusz Felisiak), backend implementations should fall back to ``super().last_executed_query()`` rather than returning ``None``.

This patch:
- Adds the fallback for Oracle.
- Matches the expected backend contract.
- Has no behavioral impact on successful queries.
- Only affects debug/inspection paths.
- Requires no new tests (ticket metadata: Needs tests: no).

This completes the cleanup after #36380.

#### Checklist

title: Fixed #36030 -- Fixed precision loss in division of Decimal literals on SQLite.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36030

#### Branch description

On SQLite, ``SQLiteNumericMixin.as_sqlite()`` currently wraps all ``DecimalField`` expressions in ``CAST(... AS NUMERIC)``, including literal ``Value(Decimal(...))`` expressions.
For these literals, the cast is unnecessary and differs from how other backends pass Decimal parameters.

The solution decouples ``Value`` from ``SQLiteNumericMixin`` by implementing a dedicated ``as_sqlite`` method that explicitly casts ``Decimal`` literals to ``REAL``, ensuring correct floating-point arithmetic is used. To prevent regressions, the implementation retains the existing ``CAST(... AS NUMERIC)`` wrapper for ``non-Decimal`` types (such as integers) when they are output to a DecimalField.

**Tests**
A new regression test, ``test_decimal_division_literal_value``, has been added to ``BasicExpressionsTests`` to verify that division yields the correct fractional precision on all backends.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch.
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" flag on the Trac ticket.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in light/dark modes for UI changes (N/A).
author: VIZZARD-X

reviews.body: This appears to fix the problem as far as I can tell. Your test passes with the fix and fails without it (which is what we want). Same is true for the repro case at the top of the ticket. I personally would have been inclined to write the test so that it's verifying the correctness of the observable outcome (along the lines of that repro case), rather than the mechanics of the solution. But I'll leave it to those higher up the food chain than I am to decide whether that should prevent the PR from being accepted and applied.
Thanks, nice start @VIZZARD-X 👍

commits.message:
Fixed #36030 -- Fixed precision loss in division of Decimal literals on SQLite.

Thanks Bob Kline for the review.

title: Fixed #36750 -- Made ordering of M2M objects deterministic in serializers.
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36750

#### Branch description

Following the implementation of `QuerySet.totally_ordered` in ticket [#36857](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/36857), this patch updates the Python and XML serializers to apply deterministic ordering for many-to-many relations using natural keys. The serializers check ``qs.totally_ordered`` and, when the ``queryset`` lacks a total ordering, they extract any existing ordering and simply append ``'pk'`` to break ties. This preserves any default model sorting while guaranteeing determinism, addressing the inconsistent fixture output on PostgreSQL without imposing redundant sorting.
 A regression test in `tests/fixtures/tests.py` verifies this behavior for both formats.

#### Checklist

title: Fixed #36751 -- Fixed empty filtered aggregation crash over annotated queryset.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36404

#### Branch description
Regression in b8e5a8a9a2a767f584cbe89a878a42363706f939.

Refs ticket-36404.

The `replace_expressions` method was innapropriately dealing with falsey but not `None` source expressions causing them to also be potentially evaluated when `__bool__` was invoked (e.g. `QuerySet.__bool__` evalutates the query).

The changes introduced in b8e5a8a9a2, which were to deal with a similar issue, surfaced the problem as aggregation over an annotated queryset requires an inlining (or pushdown) of aggregate references which is achieved through `replace_expressions`.

In cases where an empty `Q` objects was provided as an aggregate filter, such as when the admin facetting feature was used as reported, it would wrongly be turned into `None`, instead of an empty `WhereNode`, causing a crash at aggregate filter compilation.

Note that the crash signature differed depending on whether or not the backend natively supports aggregate filtering (`supports_aggregate_filter_clause`) as the fallback, which makes use `Case` / `When` expressions, would result in a `TypeError` instead of a `NoneType` `AttributeError`.

Thanks @rafaelurben for the report, Antoliny and @2ykwang for the triage.
author: charettes

reviews.body: I can confirm that this PR resolves the issue observed in ticket-36751 👍

commits.message:
Fixed #36751 -- Fixed empty filtered aggregation crash over annotated queryset.

Regression in b8e5a8a9a2a767f584cbe89a878a42363706f939.

Refs #36404.

The replace_expressions method was innapropriately dealing with falsey but not
None source expressions causing them to also be potentially evaluated when
__bool__ was invoked (e.g. QuerySet.__bool__ evaluates the queryset).

The changes introduced in b8e5a8a9a2, which were to deal with a similar issue,
surfaced the problem as aggregation over an annotated queryset requires an
inlining (or pushdown) of aggregate references which is achieved through
replace_expressions.

In cases where an empty Q object was provided as an aggregate filter, such
as when the admin facetting feature was used as reported, it would wrongly be
turned into None, instead of an empty WhereNode, causing a crash at aggregate
filter compilation.

Note that the crash signature differed depending on whether or not the backend
natively supports aggregate filtering (supports_aggregate_filter_clause) as
the fallback, which makes use Case / When expressions, would result in a
TypeError instead of a NoneType AttributeError.

Thanks Rafael Urben for the report, Antoliny and Youngkwang Yang for the triage.

title: Fixed #36620 -- Fixed workflow to summarize coverage in PRs.
body:
ref - https://github.com/django/django/pull/19777#issuecomment-3567658385

The coverage workflow was reporting "No lines with coverage information in this diff" even when PRs contained changes to Python files that were executed during tests.


since we switched to `pull_request_target`, which by default checks out the **base branch** (main) instead of the PR branch. 

### Solution

Added explicit checkout of the PR branch using `ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}`:

```yaml
- name: Checkout
  uses: actions/checkout@v5
  with:
    ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}  # ← Checkout PR code
    fetch-depth: 0
    persist-credentials: false
```
author: 0saurabh0

Thanks for the updates 👍
Looking great, after this round I'll test on my fork.
Great, this is working well!
Thanks @0saurabh0, really appreciate the extra care here.

ticket-36727

#### Branch description

The lack of ability of the get_placeholder call chain to return SQL and parameters separated so they can be mogrified by the backend at execution time forced implementations to dangerously interpolate potentially user controlled values.

The get_placeholder_sql name was chosen due to it's proximity to the previous method but other options such as Field.as_sql were considered but ultimately rejected due to its different input signature compared to Expression.as_sql that might have lead to confusion.

There is a lot of overlap between what Field.get_db_prep_value and get_placeholder_sql do but folding the latter in the former would require changing its return signature to return expression which is a way more invasive change than what is proposed here.

Given we always call get_db_prep_value it might still be an avenue worth exploring in the future to offer a publicly documented interface to allow field to take an active part in the compilation chain.
author: charettes

reviews.body: This looks great. The notes were very helpful.
Terrific!

commits.message:
Fixed #36727 -- Deprecated Field.get_placeholder in favor of get_placeholder_sql.

The lack of ability of the get_placeholder call chain to return SQL and
parameters separated so they can be mogrified by the backend at execution time
forced implementations to dangerously interpolate potentially user controlled
values.

The get_placeholder_sql name was chosen due to its proximity to the previous
method, but other options such as Field.as_sql were considered but ultimately
rejected due to its different input signature compared to Expression.as_sql
that might have lead to confusion.

There is a lot of overlap between what Field.get_db_prep_value and
get_placeholder_sql do but folding the latter in the former would require
changing its return signature to return expression which is a way more invasive
change than what is proposed here.

Given we always call get_db_prep_value it might still be an avenue worth
exploring in the future to offer a publicly documented interface to allow field
to take an active part in the compilation chain.

Thanks Jacob for the review.

author: dchest

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR improves the documentation for Django's scrypt password hasher by adding standard terminology and correcting parameter descriptions.

- Corrected `work_factor` description to specify it must be a power of 2 and maps to scrypt's *N* parameter
- Added missing `block_size` description explaining its role in tuning memory latency (scrypt's *r* parameter)
- Clarified `parallelism` to describe independent computations rather than threads (scrypt's *p* parameter)
- Enhanced memory usage guidance by noting multithreaded implementations multiply memory by parallelism

title: Fixed #36708 -- Initialized formset to None in ChangeList.__init__().
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36708

#### Branch description

This PR adds a default formset = None attribute to the ChangeList class.
The Ticket describes how when Django instantiates ChangeList through ModelAdmin, it assigns formset dynamically.
However, when developers create their own custom ChangeList instances, formset may not be set, leading to an AttributeError in templates.
Defining formset as a class-level field (attribute) makes sure that behaviour is a bit more consistent 

#### Checklist

If you have any design or process questions then you can ask in the [Django forum](https://forum.djangoproject.com/c/internals/5).

Thank you for working on this @Rudraksha-007 ⭐️

As Ben mentioned in the ticket, by defining the `formset` attribute on `ChangeList`, it seems we’ll need to update the parts in the `changelist_view` method where `formset` was being assigned dynamically.

```diff --git a/django/contrib/admin/options.py b/django/contrib/admin/options.py
index 6c202c8e61..62856cebfb 100644
--- a/django/contrib/admin/options.py
+++ b/django/contrib/admin/options.py
@@ -2050,11 +2050,6 @@ class ModelAdmin(BaseModelAdmin):
             # me back" button on the action confirmation page.
             return HttpResponseRedirect(request.get_full_path())
 
-        # If we're allowing changelist editing, we need to construct a formset
-        # for the changelist given all the fields to be edited. Then we'll
-        # use the formset to validate/process POSTed data.
-        formset = cl.formset = None
-
         # Handle POSTed bulk-edit data.
         if request.method == "POST" and cl.list_editable and "_save" in request.POST:
             if not self.has_change_permission(request):
@@ -2063,13 +2058,13 @@ class ModelAdmin(BaseModelAdmin):
             modified_objects = self._get_list_editable_queryset(
                 request, FormSet.get_default_prefix()
             )
-            formset = cl.formset = FormSet(
+            cl.formset = FormSet(
                 request.POST, request.FILES, queryset=modified_objects
             )
-            if formset.is_valid():
+            if cl.formset.is_valid():
                 changecount = 0
                 with transaction.atomic(using=router.db_for_write(self.model)):
-                    for form in formset.forms:
+                    for form in cl.formset.forms:
                         if form.has_changed():
                             obj = self.save_form(request, form, change=True)
                             self.save_model(request, obj, form, change=True)
@@ -2095,11 +2090,11 @@ class Mo

Thank you for update @Rudraksha-007 ⭐️

I left small comments :)

title: Fixed #36728 -- Validate template tag `context` argument at definition time, rather than compile time
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36728

#### Branch description

Previously, the `context` argument was validated at template compile time, rather than tag definition time. 

Because the function parsing was already done at definition time, the performance benefits are going to be miniscule at best, since the validation is simply checking list members. Therefore, benchmarking is likely to only produce noise. Similarly, because compiled templates are cached, the improvement for most projects will also be small. However, doing the work once per tag, rather than once per tag use is still better, and could make a small improvement for larger codebases.

Note that because of this eagerness, it's possible this is a breaking change, as previously invalid but unused tags will raise errors when loaded. This is likely a niche enough issue not to worry about.

#### Checklist
- [ ] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [ ] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [ ] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.

- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.

title: Fixed #36723 -- Removed logic for repositioning FilteredSelectMultiple help text.
body: #### Trac ticket number ticket-36723 #### Branch description This PR removes the JavaScript logic from SelectFilter2.js that was intended to reposition the FilteredSelectMultiple help text. This change accepts the default "below" position for the help text. #### Checklist - [X] This PR targets the `main` branch. - [X] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period. - [X] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system. - [X] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes. - [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests. - [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable. --- **Before (Light Mode):** **After (Light Mode):** **After (Dark Mode):**
author: varunkasyap

reviews.body: Thank you for working on this ticket @varunkasyap ⭐️ I was curious why, in the past, we tried to position the `help_text` for `FilterSelect` above the widget, so I looked it up. In the above photo, you can see the Django 1.8 admin, where the `help_text` used to be located below the `related_widget_wrapper`. I figured that, as the comment suggests, the `help_text` was probably moved up to address these kinds of issues. In my opinion, moving the `help_text` above the current widget now might feel a bit awkward. Unlike other `help_texts`, placing only the `FilterSelectWidget` `help_text` above the widget would create a consistency issue. It would make sense if the default height of the `FilterSelectWidget` were very tall, but that’s not the case. As shown above, it’s the same help_text, but the position is different. Additionally, if we move the help_text upward, it seems we would also need to adjust a few styles. How about keeping the `help_text` as it is instead of moving it above the `FilterSelectWidget`, and removing the related code?
Thanks @varunkasyap! And thanks @Antoliny0919 for the review.

commits.message: Fixed #36723 -- Removed logic for repositioning FilteredSelectMultiple help text.

title: Fixed #36717 -- Redirect authenticated users on admin login view to next URL.
body:
Admin login should redirect already logged-in users to page specified in next parameter

#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36717

#### Branch description
 The admin login page at `/admin/login/` ignores the `?next=` parameter when an already logged-in user visits the page. This differs from the behaviour when the user logs in on the admin login page itself, where they are redirected to the page indicated in the parameter.

I've added tests for the behaviour and ported checks from [`contrib.auth.views.RedirectURLMixin`](https://github.com/django/django/blob/77d455ae73b177a32102f0b248828b5d63c0aa24/django/contrib/auth/views.py#L48-L56) to ensure the URL is safe. I did not port is the overridable `success_url_allowed_hosts` - I'm not sure how we could do that here (except for adding to `success_url_allowed_hosts` to `AdminSite`).

Fixes a `TypeError` crash in the `intcomma` filter when it receives non-finite `Decimal` values like `Infinity`, `-Infinity`, or `NaN`.

title: Fixed #36710 -- Fixed a regression in urlize for multi-part domain names.
body:
#### Trac ticket number


ticket-36710

#### Branch description

This PR fixes a regression in `django.utils.html.urlize` where valid domain-like strings (e.g., `as.d8f.ghj8.gov`) are no longer recognized as URLs.
The issue was introduced after changes in URL validation logic (see [commit a9fe98d5](https://github.com/django/django/commit/a9fe98d5bd4212d069afe8316101984aadecfbb2#diff-7c4f4486e07496c325bac53eb0f814a979edbfffc8c48877ddbae83120ab3c84R299-R301)
), which modified the hostname regex used by `urlize()`.

#### Checklist
- [X] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [X] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [X] I have added or updated relevant tests.

title: Refs #36680 -- Avoided manipulating PATH in AdminScriptTestCase.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36680

#### Branch description
This mostly reverts 6436ec321073bf0622af815e0af08f54c97f9b30, which was fragile. Instead, if `black` is present, we use it to format the expected result, instead of hard-coding the expected formatted value.

See #20034 and #20054 for problems with the prior approach, including comments there about even further cases revealed on the no-requirements build where a path component could be `None`.

Thanks @h4l and @jonbiemond for reports.
#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [n/a] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [x] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: jacobtylerwalls

reviews.body: Thank you for this work! Added some feedback, LMKWYT!

This looks great, thank you so much @jacobtylerwalls! 🌟 

For reference to others, this is a partial revert of 6436ec321073bf0622af815e0af08f54c97f9b30 and the diff compared to the original code looks like this:
```diff
diff --git a/tests/admin_scripts/tests.py b/tests/admin_scripts/tests.py
index 3718354dc7..0c27194568 100644
--- a/tests/admin_scripts/tests.py
+++ b/tests/admin_scripts/tests.py
@@ -169,6 +169,16 @@ class AdminScriptTestCase(SimpleTestCase):
 
         return self.run_test(["./manage.py", *args], settings_file)
 
+    def assertInAfterFormatting(self, member, container, msg=None):
+        if HAS_BLACK:
+            import black
+
+            # Black does not have a stable API, but this is still less fragile
+            # than attempting to filter out all paths where it is available.
+            member = black.format_str(member, mode=black.FileMode())
+
+        self.assertIn(member, container, msg=msg)
+
     def assertNoOutput(self, stream):
         "Utility assertion: assert that the given stream is empty"
         self.assertEqual(
@@ -744,10 +754,7 @@ class DjangoAdminSettingsDirectory(AdminScriptTestCase):
         with open(os.path.join(app_path, "apps.py")) as f:
             content = f.read()
             self.assertIn("class SettingsTestConfig(AppConfig)", content)
-            self.assertIn(
-                'name = "settings_test"' if HAS_BLACK else "name = 'settings_test'",
-                content,
-            )
+            self.assertInAfterFormatting("name = 'settings_test'", content)
 
     def test_setup_environ_custom_template(self):
         """
@@ -772,9 +779,7 @@ class DjangoAdminSettingsDirectory(AdminScriptTestCase):
         with open(os.path.join(app_path, "apps.py"), encoding="utf8") as f:
             content = f.read()
             self.assertIn("class こんにちはConfig(AppConfig)", content)
-            self.assertIn(
-                'name = "こんにちは"' if HAS_BLACK else "name = 'こんにちは'", content
-            )
+

commits.message:
Refs #36680 -- Avoided manipulating PATH in AdminScriptTestCase.

This mostly reverts 6436ec321073bf0622af815e0af08f54c97f9b30,
which was fragile. Instead, if black is present, we use it to format the
expected and actual results, instead of hard-coding the expected
formatted value.

Co-authored-by: Natalia <124304+nessita@users.noreply.github.com>

title: Fixed #36704 -- Fixed check errors for proxy subclass with a composite pk.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36704

#### Branch description

Proxy models subclassing a model with a CompositePrimaryKey were
incorrectly reporting check errors because the check that requires only
local fields to be used in a composite pk was evaluated against the proxy
subclass, which has no fields.

To fix this we now do not evaluate any composite pk field checks against
proxy subclasses, as none of the checks are applicable to proxy
subclasses. This also has the benefit of not double-reporting real check
errors from an invalid superclass pk.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.

commits.message:
Fixed #36704 -- Fixed system check error for proxy model with a composite pk.

Proxy models subclassing a model with a CompositePrimaryKey were
incorrectly reporting check errors because the check that requires only
local fields to be used in a composite pk was evaluated against the proxy
subclass, which has no fields.

To fix this, composite pk field checks are not evaluated against
proxy subclasses, as none of the checks are applicable to proxy
subclasses. This also has the benefit of not double-reporting real check
errors from an invalid superclass pk.

Thanks Clifford Gama for the review.

body:
In french we need to specialize 1st which does not work like 81st, it's 1<sup>er</sup> and 81<sup>ième</sup>. For zero is tricky but it's attested that some use 0<sup>ième</sup>.

Also fixed 101 that was tested to be `101er` while in french it's `101e` (sourced in my commit).

Thank you @JulienPalard for this work! 🌟 

I'm happy to land once the nitpick in the comment is sorted, but also we need to revert all changes to `.po` and `.mo` files. We handle translations enterily in Transifex and we update the `.po`, all at once, before the string freeze which is next week.

cc/ @claudep for awarness
Thanks, looks good! (after changes required by @nessita are done)

Thank you @JulienPalard! I finally went back to this PR, thank you for your patience 🌟 

I changed the tests so they don't depend on the main's Django .po and .mo files, I followed the same pattern that the i18n tests use. Once this lands, and after 6.1 release candidate is confirmed, the translation for French needs to be updated in Transifex.

commits.message:
Refs #28877 -- Added special ordinal context when humanizing value 1.

Some languages use a different ordinal suffix for the number 1 than
for other values ending in 1 (e.g. 21, 31). Added a dedicated
pgettext context "ordinal is 1" to allow translators to handle
this distinction.

For example, in French, 1 is written as "1er" while 21, 31, etc. use
"21e", "31e", etc.

Co-authored-by: Natalia <124304+nessita@users.noreply.github.com>

title: Fixed #36696 -- Resolved NameError with PY314 when DEBUG=True
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36696

#### Branch description

This PR resolves `NameError`s with Python 3.14 when using annotations guarded by `if TYPE_CHECKING` in application code due to deferred evaluation.

With `DEBUG=True`, Django will perform inspection of signals and templatetags during registration to check if they accept kwargs etc. With Python 3.14, this triggers evaluation of annotations for these signals or templatetags, which may not be possible if the annotations are (as usual) written for static type checkers only, leading to `NameError`. This PR avoids this by using `annotation_format=annotationlib.Format.FORWARDREF`, which is available since Python 3.14.

#### Checklist
- [X] This PR targets the `main` branch.
- [X] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [X] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [X] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [x] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: prauscher

reviews.body: Thanks again for the report! I pushed tiny edits. Keep the 3.14 issues coming! 🏆

commits.message:
Fixed #36696 -- Fixed NameError when inspecting functions with deferred annotations.

In Python 3.14, annotations are deferred by default, so we should not
assume that the names in them have been imported unconditionally.

title: Fixed #36321 -- Added argparse suggest_on_error support for Python 3.14+
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36321

#### Branch description
This PR enables `argparse's suggest_on_error feature` for Django management commands when running on Python 3.14 or higher. This provides helpful suggestions when users mistype command arguments.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: kihuni

reviews.body:
Thank you for working on this! Go go Djangonauts!

We'd also like to affect the `parser` created in `django/core/management/__init__.py:ManagementUtility.execute`, for `manage.py` usage.

Also, we need to ensure subparsers are affected, as created in `CommandParser.add_subparsers()`.

I think it might be better to hoist defaulting the argument on in `CommandParser.__init__()`, so all call sites are affected.

Thanks for iterating. Here is another round of review!

I think I misunderstood the feature when writing up the ticket, and that's carried on in your implementation. Take a look at my comment in the test file. It would be good if you could also run a management command locally on Python 3.14+ with this branch, to see the suggestions working.
Looking good, just one last detail.
Thanks @kihuni! I rebased and pushed only minor edits to comments. Welcome aboard ⛵ !

commits.message:
Fixed #36321 -- Defaulted suggest_on_error=True in management commands.

Python 3.15 defaults suggest_on_error=True, but the feature is available
from 3.14, so this change opts in earlier. This change can be reverted
when Python 3.15 is the minimum supported version.

title: Fixed #36596 -- Made parallel test runner respect django_test_skips and django_test_expected_failures.
body: ticket-36596
author: felixxm

reviews.body:
Thanks @felixxm. What do you think about a refactor to reduce the duplication in the SQLite connection? There would be a few ways to do this, but this one avoids so much start method checking:

```diff
diff --git a/django/db/backends/base/creation.py b/django/db/backends/base/creation.py
index 98e7cc8023..7c11465f94 100644
--- a/django/db/backends/base/creation.py
+++ b/django/db/backends/base/creation.py
@@ -1,4 +1,3 @@
-import multiprocessing
 import os
 import sys
 import warnings
@@ -403,10 +402,3 @@ class BaseDatabaseCreation:
         # to the default database instead of the appropriate clone.
         self.connection.settings_dict.update(settings_dict)
         self.connection.close()
-
-        start_method = multiprocessing.get_start_method()
-        if (
-            start_method in {"forkserver", "spawn"}
-            and os.environ.get("RUNNING_DJANGOS_TEST_SUITE") == "true"
-        ):
-            self.mark_expected_failures_and_skips()

Fixed #36689 -- Fixed top-level JSONField __in lookup failures on MySQL and Oracle.

Added a JSONIn lookup to handle correct serialization and extraction
for JSONField top-level __in queries on backends without native JSON
support. KeyTransformIn now subclasses JSONIn.

Co-authored-by: Jacob Walls <jacobtylerwalls@gmail.com>

Thanks Jacob Walls for the report and review.
Refs #36689 -- Serialized JSONIn rhs parameters wrapped in Value expressions.

Refs #36025 -- Made get_prep_lookup() pass output_field when wrapping direct values in Value.

Previously, only strings were supplied with an output_field when wrapping
direct value iterable elements in Value expressions for ExpressionList. This
caused problems for __in lookups on JSONField when using expressions
alongside direct values, as JSONField values can have different types which
need to be adapted by the field's get_db_prep_value().

Refs #36689.

Thanks Jacob Walls for the review.

title: Fixed #36686 -- Clarified Meta.ordering is ignored in GROUP BY queries.
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36686

#### Branch description

This PR addresses ticket #36686 by clarifying that a model's `Meta.ordering` (default ordering) is not respected in `GROUP BY` queries, such as those using `.values()` and `.annotate()`.

The current documentation was misleading, particularly the note stating Django "never removes ordering constraints," which caused confusion.

This patch fixes the documentation in three key places:
1.  `docs/topics/db/aggregation.txt`: Clarifies the "never removes" note to apply only to explicit `.order_by()` calls.
~~2.  `docs/ref/models/querysets.txt`: Corrects a related misleading note in the `distinct()` documentation, as pointed out in comment 3.~~
3.  `docs/ref/models/options.txt`: Adds a new warning note directly to the `Meta.ordering` definition itself.

#### Checklist
- [X] This PR targets the `main` branch. 
- [X] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [X] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.

- [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.

title: Fixed #36674 -- Prevented memory leak in select_related().
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36674

#### Branch description
Fixed memory leak in select_related() by converting the nested recursive 
function get_select_from_parent to a class method.

The nested function created closure cells that caused circular references,
preventing Python's garbage collector from properly cleaning up memory.

Implemented classmethod approach as suggested by core developers for better
recursion handling with cls.get_select_from_parent().

#### Checklist
- [x] Commit message follows Django conventions
- [x] No regressions in select_related tests
- [ ] "Has patch" flag will be set after PR creation

reviews.body: Looking good, just want to ensure we leverage the existing approach that asserts against `gc.garbage`.

Thanks for the updates. I pushed small edits to test code.

If and when we reuse the util we can move it to django/test/utils.py. 👍

title: Fixed #36678 -- Propagated errors from _init_worker in ParallelTestRunner.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
ticket-36678

#### Branch description
Before, errors during `_init_worker` were swallowed, leading to infinite retries via the multiprocessing pool. See ticket-36677 for an example of a failure that caused infinite retries.

Now, errors during `_init_worker` are raised in a way that causes the ParallelTestRunner to exit.

Errors during `_init_worker` would have been largely theoretical before ticket-36083 (6.0), but with that change, we run system checks during `_init_worker`, which can fail. The checks run once before workers are forked, so for deterministic checks, you wouldn't reach this failure point, but with flaky checks, a user might end up in an infinite retry scenario similar to ticket-36677.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: jacobtylerwalls

reviews.body: Looks good, though I have some concerns/questions. I may be threading the needle too finely 😛
Looks great, thank you! 🌟

Fixed #36678 -- Limited retries in ParallelTestRunner.

Thanks Natalia Bidart for the review.

title: Made RemoteTestResultTest.test_pickle_errors_detection() compatible with tblib 3.2+.
body:
`tblib` 3.2.0+ makes exception subclasses with `__init__()` and the default `___reduce__()` picklable. Therefore, `RemoteTestResult._confirm_picklable()` doesn't raise exceptions for exception subclasses that do this when `tblib` 3.2.0+ is installed.

https://github.com/ionelmc/python-tblib/blob/master/CHANGELOG.rst#320-2025-10-21
author: felixxm

commits.message:
Made RemoteTestResultTest.test_pickle_errors_detection() compatible with tblib 3.2+.

tblib 3.2+ makes exception subclasses with __init__() and the default
__reduce__() picklable. This broke the test for
RemoteTestResult._confirm_picklable(), which expects a specific
exception to fail unpickling.

https://github.com/ionelmc/python-tblib/blob/master/CHANGELOG.rst#320-2025-10-21

This fix defines ExceptionThatFailsUnpickling.__reduce__() in a way
that pickle.dumps(obj) succeeds, but pickle.loads(pickle.dumps(obj))
raises TypeError.

Refs #27301. This preserves the intent of the regression test from
52188a5ca6bafea0a66f17baacb315d61c7b99cd without skipping it.

title: Fixed #36665 -- Improved manager usage guidance in optimization docs
body:
Replaced vague 'tricky' language with specific examples of when managers are not used: instance access (raises AttributeError) and related field access that may bypass custom manager methods. Added reference to queries documentation for more details.

This addresses the ticket's request to answer "when wouldn't my model manager be used?" with concrete examples instead of leaving readers with the impression of information hiding.

#### Trac ticket number
36665

#### Branch description
This branch fixes ticket #36665 by replacing vague "tricky" language in the database optimization documentation with specific examples of when Django model managers are not used. The change provides concrete examples (instance access raising AttributeError and related field access bypassing custom managers) and links to the queries documentation for more details.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch.
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: segnimekonnen7

reviews.body:
Thanks for the PR. I don't think the AttributeError you mention here nor model methods are what's being alluded to.

I have to confess I'm not sure what is being alluded to here, which is why I opened the ticket. We need to find out what's being alluded to. This needs a little investigation.

One guess I have is that it's alluding to the situation when default managers are used in lieu of base managers:https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/5.2/topics/db/managers/#using-managers-for-related-object-access

I would accept a PR that replaced the tricky wording with a summary of this distinction linking to that portion of the docs.
Thanks for the updates.

title: Skipped GISFunctionsTests.test_geometry_type() test for MultiPoint on MariaDB and GEOS 3.12+.
body:
`GEOSWKTWriter_write()` behavior was changed in GEOS 3.12+ to include parentheses for sub-members (https://github.com/libgeos/geos/pull/903).
    
MariaDB doesn't accept WKT representations with additional parentheses for `MultiPoint`. This is an accepted bug ([MDEV-36166](https://jira.mariadb.org/browse/MDEV-36166)) in MariaDB that should be fixed in the future.
author: felixxm

reviews.body: Thanks for the sleuthing and debugging session @felixxm 🙇

commits.message: Moved object creation to subTest() in GISFunctionsTests.test_geometry_type() test.

Skipped GISFunctionsTests.test_geometry_type() test for MultiPoint on MariaDB and GEOS 3.12+.

GEOSWKTWriter_write() behavior was changed in GEOS 3.12+ to include
parentheses for sub-members (https://github.com/libgeos/geos/pull/903).

MariaDB doesn't accept WKT representations with additional parentheses
for MultiPoint. This is an accepted bug (MDEV-36166) in MariaDB that
should be fixed in the future:

- https://jira.mariadb.org/browse/MDEV-36166

title: Removed setuptools from contributor docs and GitHub actions.
body:
These commits can be evaluated separately.

1 - remove mention of setuptools from contributor docs.

The presence of setuptools here is, I think, a mistake. Below this list it is claimed that these dependencies will be installed via:
```py
python -m pip install -r tests/requirements/py3.txt
```
Which is not true for setuptools, it's only for packaging.

2 - remove setuptools from github actions

The presence of setuptools here is overly defensive. It also struck Florian as strange in https://github.com/django/django/pull/15522#discussion_r829753129.

With the 6.1 feature freeze 7 months away, I think it's a good time to try removing it.
author: jacobtylerwalls

reviews.body:
LGTM, thank you! 🌟 

Personally, I wouldn't go as far as removing `setuptools`. What it's proposed is already a nice (and likely safe) cleanup.

commits.message: Removed mention of setuptools in docs/internals/contributing/writing-code/unit-tests.txt.

Removed setuptools from GitHub actions.

Follow-up to 61c5c3173281b1e906a891aa6a6c6f9cdc9f2b8a.

title: Fixed #31506 -- Documented DateField and timedelta behavior with ExpressionWrapper.
body:
Add warning in ExpressionWrapper documentation about date/datetime type differences when using timedelta on PostgreSQL and MySQL. Included Cast() and integer arithmetic solutions.

#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-31506

#### Branch description
It documents the behavioral differences across database backends when using DateField with timedelta inside an ExpressionWrapper.

To address the issue where arithmetic operations on date/time fields in PostgreSQL and MySQL can return a datetime type instead of a date, a warning has been added to the ExpressionWrapper documentation. It also provides solutions, such as using the Cast() function, to help developers avoid unintended type mismatches.

#### Checklist
- [X] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [X] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [X] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [X] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [X] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: forwardyoung

reviews.body:
Thanks for the PR! Will be great to add help here to prevent paper cuts. 👍 

A concern I have is that adding this level of detail to the `ExpressionWrapper` section could reinforce a misunderstanding about what it does.

The problem surfaces when using simpler expressions like `F()`, as in your example. The reporter expected this problem to be solved via `ExpressionWrapper`, but that was a misunderstanding. `ExpressionWrapper` only helps you set `output_field`, which is an ORM concept[0], not a database concept, on expressions that don't have `output_field`. If we are going to add admonitions to a tool's docs about when *not* to use that tool, I'd prefer the advice be general instead of highly specialized to date fields.

The advice you present here to use `Cast` or integer arithmetic or custom fields is correct, but we could present that in the `DateField` docs instead, where there are similar warnings about other paper cuts, rather than detouring into `ExpressionWrapper`.

That said, I do think we should clarify in the `ExpressionWrapper` docs that it doesn't do any casting in the database.

So, to pull it all together, my recommendation is to:
- clarify in `ExpressionWrapper` how it differs from `Cast`
- move the advice about creating a custom model field to the docs for `output_field`, where it suggests `output_field` is "only required when Django is unable to automatically determine the result’s field type". That's true, but it is *also helpful* in cases where you want to encapsulate the python-side casting behavior, which you can do with custom fields, which you point out here very well.
- move the admonition about postgres/mysql datefield arithmetic behavior to the `DateField` admonitions, keeping your integer arithmetic alternative example

Thanks again, and welcome aboard! ⛵ 

***
[0] - The fact that in at least one case `output_field` actually [entails a database cast](https://github.com/django/django/pull/19459#discussion_r2135725928) is
Thank you, updates look great, just some last edits. Also mind fixing the merge conflicts?
Thank you @forwardyoung! Should be good to go once the missing backticks and line lengths lints are fixed 👍
Yay, checks are passing now. I gave this a final read and noticed one slight inconsistency, can you let me know if you agree with this suggestion?
Great, the latest rounds of changes read perfectly. Thanks for being so responsive. Welcome aboard! ⛵

commits.message:
Fixed #31506 -- Clarified that ExpressionWrapper does not perform database casts.

Added warning in DateField documentation about type differences when using
timedelta on PostgreSQL and MySQL. Mentioned Cast() and integer arithmetic
solutions.

title: Optimized View.dispatch() a bit.
body:
Doing:
- attribute fetching of `.method` and `.lower`
- `lower()` calls

are not cheap, especially `.lower()` part.

```python
» pyperf timeit -s 'method = "PATCH"' 'method.lower()'
.....................

Mean +- std dev: 19.9 ns +- 0.2 ns
```

There's no need to do it twice in the hotest code path. `LOAD_NAME` is faster:

```python
» pyperf timeit -s 'method = "PATCH".lower()' 'method'
.....................

Mean +- std dev: 3.90 ns +- 0.05 ns
```

Since there are no downsides to it, I propose to make all requests a bit faster for free.

#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-XXXXX

title: Fixed #36646 -- Added compatibility for oracledb 3.4.0.
body:
#### Trac ticket number
<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write "N/A" if this is a trivial PR. -->

ticket-36646

#### Branch description

The `Database.Binary`, `Date`, and `Timestamp` attributes were changed from aliases of `bytes`, `datetime.date`, and `datetime.datetime` to factory functions in oracle/python-oracledb@869a887819cdac7fcd610f9d9d463ade49ea7, which made their usage inadequate in `isinstance` checks.

Thanks @jowage58 for the report and Natalia for the triage.
author: charettes

commits.message:
Fixed #36646 -- Added compatibility for oracledb 3.4.0.

The Database.Binary, Date, and Timestamp attributes were changed from
aliases to bytes, datetime.date, and datetime.datetime to factory
functions in oracle/python-oracledb@869a887819cdac7fcd610f9d9d463ade49ea7
which made their usage inadequate for isinstance checks.

Thanks John Wagenleitner for the report and Natalia for the triage.

Co-authored-by: Mariusz Felisiak <felisiak.mariusz@gmail.com>

title: Pinned "New contributor" GitHub action to v3.0 to fix "Error: Input required and not supplied: issue_message".
body:
#### Trac ticket number
N/A

#### Branch description
This change fixes the ["Hello new contributor" GitHub Action](https://github.com/django/django/blob/main/.github/workflows/new_contributor_pr.yml), which started failing due to a breaking change introduced in `actions/first-interaction` version 3.1. In this version, the `issue_message` field became a required part of the configuration.

At this time, it’s unclear whether this requirement is intentional or a mistake, as it was not officially announced and is currently being discussed in [this issue](https://github.com/actions/first-interaction/issues/365).

To resolve the problem, the action has been pinned to version 3.0, which is known to work reliably.
author: timonweb

reviews.body: Thank you so much @timonweb! I'll wait until tomorrow before merging to allow GH to fix this. But well done on pointing to the issue! :star2:

commits.message: Added required "issue_message" input to "New contributor" GitHub action.

title: Fixed #36468 -- Fixed failure to close popup when adding a related object in the admin.
body:
…t is managed by SelectBox.js before trying to update the cache

#### Trac ticket number

ticket-36468

#### Branch description

If `filter_horizontal` is used, the `SelectBox.js` is loaded which manages the state of the filter widget. After a popup form is closed, all related selects are updated. It fails for selects that aren't used as a filter widget, because a check is missing, if the `SelectBox` is actually responsible for the select.

title: Fixed #35729 -- Enabled natural key serialization opt-out for subclasses.
body:
Refactored the serialization logic to allow models inheriting a natural_key() method (e.g., AbstractBaseUser subclasses) to explicitly opt out of natural key serialization by returning an empty tuple ().

The fix ensures that:
1. Primary keys are not omitted from the serialized object dump.
2. Foreign keys fall back to standard integer PK serialization instead of list-based natural key formats.

This addresses a usability regression where subclasses could not cancel natural key inheritance. Thanks Jonas Dittrich for the report

#### Trac ticket number
ticket-35729

#### Branch description
This branch implements a robust opt-out mechanism for natural key serialization in Django.

_Issue Overview_
Models that inherit the natural_key method (like subclasses of AbstractBaseUser) were previously unable to prevent Django's serialization framework from omitting their primary key (pk) when dumping data with natural_primary_keys=True. This occurred because the framework only checked for the method's existence rather than its result, leading to data loss upon deserialization.

_Proposed Changes_
The serialization logic in PythonSerializer and XMLSerializer has been refactored to check the return value of natural_key(). The system now treats the following as explicit opt-out signals, forcing a fallback to standard integer PK serialization:

return () (An empty/falsy value).

This ensures that the primary key is always included in the dump, and Foreign Keys are serialized as standard integers, fully resolving the usability bug.

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [x] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [x] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: rimi0108

reviews.body:
Wonderful, thanks for taking this on. 🏅 

Left a few comments to progress this. Let me know if anything doesn't make sense.

Welcome aboard! ⛵

Thanks, this is looking very close!

Left some polish comments to get this to a smaller diff, and then from there we should be good to go. 🚀 

EDIT: when you can, please also update the body of the PR to account for the fact that only `()` is expected now as the opt-out sentinel.

Thanks @rimi0108! I appreciate you pursuing the simplifications I suggested. With those in place, it was easier to do one more round of simplifications, reducing the size of your diff even more. Well done!

commits.message:
Fixed #35729 -- Enabled natural key serialization opt-out for subclasses.

Refactored serialization logic to allow models inheriting a natural_key()
method (e.g. AbstractBaseUser) to explicitly opt out of natural key
serialization by returning an empty tuple from the method.

Thanks Jonas Dittrich for the report.

Co-authored-by: Jacob Walls <jacobtylerwalls@gmail.com>

title: Fixed #36636 -- Removed session-based storage reference from set_language() docs and docstring.
body:
This PR fixes outdated references in the documentation and docstring of django.views.i18n.set_language().
Since Django 4.0, the view no longer stores the chosen language in the session. It only uses the cookie defined by the LANGUAGE_COOKIE_NAME setting.
The docstring and docs have been updated accordingly to avoid confusion.

Thanks to Samriddha Kumar Tripathi and kid_alan_ on Discord for the report

title: Fixed #36663 -- Defaulted to running checks against all databases.
body:
Regression in 0b83c8cc4db95812f1e15ca19d78614e94cf38dd which added support for running checks only against databases but also defaulted to running against no databases if not specified.

Thanks Tim Graham for surfacing how this was a problematic default.

ticket-36663
author: charettes

commits.message:
Fixed #36663 -- Defaulted to running checks against all databases.

Regression in 0b83c8cc4db95812f1e15ca19d78614e94cf38dd which added
support for running checks only against databases but also defaulted to
running against no databases if not specified.

Note that this continues to *not* default to runing database tagged
checks for all management commands except the migrate one as whether or
not we should change this must be discussed further.

Thanks Tim Graham for surfacing how this was a problematic default.

title: Skipped NOT NULL constraints on PostgreSQL 18+.
body:
PostgreSQL 18+ stores column "NOT NULL" specifications in `pg_constraint`.

https://www.postgresql.org/docs/release/18.0/

Alternative to #19908.
author: felixxm

reviews.body:
This PR introduces a change to Django's database backend to skip the validation of NOT NULL constraints when running on PostgreSQL 18 and above. This is in response to PostgreSQL 18's introduction of the NOT VALID option for NOT NULL constraints, which allows for the addition of such constraints without immediately validating existing rows.

It looks forward compatible and needs minimal code change.
Suggested: add changes in the documentation as well
Thanks for giving this approach a shot!

commits.message:
Skipped NOT NULL constrainsts on PostgreSQL 18+.

PostgreSQL 18+ stores column "NOT NULL" specifications in pg_constraint.

https://www.postgresql.org/docs/release/18.0/

title: Fixed #35877, Refs #36128 -- Documented unique constraint when migrating a m2m field to use a through model.
body:
Trac ticket number
ticket-35877

Branch description
This PR updates the migration documentation to clarify behavior when switching from an implicit ManyToManyField to a custom through model. Django does not automatically preserve the unique index on foreign keys in this transition, which can lead to subtle bugs across database backends. To maintain consistent behavior, a UniqueConstraint should be added manually. This note aims to prevent confusion and improve migration reliability for contributors and users.

title: Refs #28526 -- Provided URLResolver namespace in technical 404 template.
body:
#### Trac ticket number

ticket-26886 ticket-28526

#### Branch description
Before, the technical 404 view was written in a way that interrogates a possibly missing `.name` on a url pattern or resolver. `.name` only exists on `URLPattern`.

Now, we log something else helpful for `URLResolver`, namely `.namespace`. This takes care of the huge traceback cited in the issue.

A custom template tag might have been a nicer implementation than what I ended up with, but the technical 404 template is not inside an app, so we can't register a custom tag AFIACT.

Thanks @cliffordgama this is such a help. I see this is in "draft", but it was in the patch review queue and looked pretty complete, so I did a full review. I have some minor feedback, a couple test ideas, and an edge case for `ArrayField` to ask you about.
Thank you @cliffordgama, this looks great! 🥇

commits.message:
Fixed #35381 -- Added JSONNull() expression.

Thanks Jacob Walls for the review.

Refs #35381 -- Delegated ArrayField element prepping to base_field.get_db_prep_save.

Previously, ArrayField always used base_field.get_db_prep_value when saving,
which could differ from how base_field prepares data for save. This change
overrides ArrayField.get_db_prep_save to delegate to the base_field's
get_db_prep_save, ensuring elements like None in JSONField arrays are saved
correctly as SQL NULL instead of JSON null.

Refs #35381 -- Deprecated using None in JSONExact rhs to mean JSON null.

Key and index lookups are exempt from the deprecation.

Co-authored-by: Jacob Walls <jacobtylerwalls@gmail.com>
Refs #35381 -- Clarified key and index lookup handling of None in exact lookup docs.

title: Fixed #36620 -- Added coverage workflow to comment coverage summary.
body:
Part of GSoC'25

ticket-36620


This PR adds a new GitHub Actions workflow i.e. `coverage.yml` for testing coverage reporting with diff-cover analysis.

### What it does:
- Runs coverage analysis on PRs (excluding docs/markdown changes)
- Generates diff coverage reports for changed lines
- Posts coverage comments on PRs (updating previous comments on every new commit)
- Keeps main CI unaffected while trialing coverage features

### Links:
- [Forum discussion](https://forum.djangoproject.com/t/rfc-prioritizing-tasks-for-automate-processes-within-django-contribution-workflow-gsoc-2025/41960)
- [Testing PR where this was developed](https://github.com/0saurabh0/django/pull/6)

### Implementation:
- Single workflow file with minimal permissions
- Concurrency control to prevent duplicate runs
- Non-blocking coverage warnings only

#### Checklist
- [x] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->
- [ ] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.
- [x] I have checked the "Has patch" ticket flag in the Trac system.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant tests.
- [ ] I have added or updated relevant docs, including release notes if applicable.
- [ ] I have attached screenshots in both light and dark modes for any UI changes.
author: 0saurabh0

reviews.body:
Thank you for the PR

In the [forum I recently mentioned](https://forum.djangoproject.com/t/rfc-prioritizing-tasks-for-automate-processes-within-django-contribution-workflow-gsoc-2025/41960/9) that I think it's worth adding some information on this in our contributing docs

Perhaps we should update the existing code coverage docs in the contributing section to add information on this GitHub action: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/5.2/internals/contributing/writing-code/unit-tests/#code-coverage
Can't wait to land this -- thanks for your help here!
Thanks again!

title: Clarify EmailValidator validates an email **address**
body:
This confused me a bit at first, because it is also possible to validate an email body (`X-Mailer:...\nSubject: ...`). Added the word `address`.

#### Checklist
